title: What to know about Schisandra chinensis before using it medicinally
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Schisandra chinensis are its application in treating fatigue due to its adaptogenic properties, which help to balance the body's physiological response to stress, as well as its ability to improve liver function and reduce oxidative stress. This plant has also been traditionally used to alleviate anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as serotonin and dopamine, thereby exerting a tranquilizing effect. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its efficacy in treating digestive issues, including irritable bowel syndrome and gastritis. Furthermore, its antioxidant and anti-amnesic properties make it useful in addressing memory loss by protecting against neuronal damage and enhancing cognitive function. The plant's immunomodulatory and anti-tumor properties have been exploited in the treatment of cancer, while its ability to regulate blood pressure and reduce cardiovascular strain makes it beneficial in managing hypertension; its role in preventing anemia by enhancing iron absorption and reducing oxidative stress is also notable, as well as its effectiveness in treating cough due to its expectorant properties, and its potential in treating cardiovascular disease and liver disease due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Schisandra chinensis are attributed to its antioxidant activity, which is partly due to the presence of lignans and phenolic acids. This plant exhibits anti-inflammatory effects, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and its antimicrobial properties are attributed to the presence of saponins and flavonoids, which inhibit the growth of various microorganisms. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis has cardioprotective effects, as it inhibits the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein cholesterol and reduces the production of angiotensin-converting enzyme. The antiviral and neuroprotective properties of Schisandra chinensis are attributed to its ability to inhibit the replication of viruses and protect against neurodegenerative diseases, respectively, due to the presence of triterpenes and lignans. Furthermore, this plant has hepatoprotective, immunomodulatory, and anxiolytic effects, protecting the liver from damage, modulating the immune system, and reducing anxiety, respectively, due to its content of glycosides and phenolic acids.
identification: Schisandra chinensis is an evergreen woody vine native to East Asia, specifically China, Japan, Korea, and parts of Russia. Its botanical characteristics include clusters of small, yellowish-green flowers and red or purple berries, with five-lobed leaves that are 3-10 cm long and 2-5 cm wide. This plant typically grows in mountainous forests, often as a climber, and prefers well-drained soil and a cool, humid climate. It is commonly found in the mountain ranges of the Himalayas, the Japanese islands, and the Korean Peninsula, as well as in the provinces of northeastern China. The species has been naturalized in some regions, including the northeastern United States and Canada.
cultivation: Schisandra chinensis is a deciduous woody vine native to East Asia, known for its complex growth habits as it can grow as a groundcover, a climbing vine, or a shrub, typically reaching 3-10 meters in height. This plant thrives in well-drained acidic soils with a pH range of 5.5-6.5 and requires full sun to partial shade, with temperatures ranging from 10-30°C, and annual precipitation of at least 800 mm. To promote healthy growth, Schisandra chinensis should be planted in a location with good air circulation, and regular watering is necessary to maintain a consistent moisture level, avoiding overwatering which can lead to root rot. Additionally, regular pruning is recommended to control the plant's size and encourage new growth, and fertilization with a balanced fertilizer can also enhance its overall health. It is essential to note that Schisandra chinensis is typically harvested after 5-7 years of cultivation, when the plant is in its prime.
history: Schisandra chinensis is a traditional Chinese medicine used for centuries to treat various health conditions. The plant's fruit, also known as the "five-flavored berry," is rich in bioactive compounds that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Schisandra chinensis is used to tonify the liver and kidneys, improve cognitive function, and enhance physical performance. Historical records indicate that it was used to treat conditions such as fatigue, insomnia, and gastrointestinal disorders, as well as to promote longevity and reduce the risk of age-related diseases. Studies have also shown that the plant's bioactive compounds may have antiviral and anticancer properties, making it a potential adjunct therapy for certain medical conditions.

constituents.name: Schisandrins
constituents.description: Schisandrins are lignans, specifically a mixture of isomeric tetrahydrofurofuran lignans with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and potentially hepatoprotective properties.

constituents.name: Saponins
constituents.description: Saponins are a type of bioactive compound found in Schisandra chinensis, characterized by their ability to form soap-like foams in water, exhibit hemolytic activity, and display cytotoxic effects on certain cell lines.

constituents.name: Flavonoids
constituents.description: Flavonoids are present in Schisandra chinensis and exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-boosting properties.

constituents.name: Phenolic acids
constituents.description: Phenolic acids are a class of compounds in Schisandra chinensis that exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents.name: Terpenoids
constituents.description: Schisandra chinensis Terpenoids are known for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-microbial properties.

constituents.name: Glycosides
constituents.description: Glycosides found in Schisandra chinensis are known to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Schisandrin B
constituents.description: Schisandrin B is a lignan sesquiterpene that exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-cancer properties.

constituents.name: Coumarins
constituents.description: Coumarins are a type of lignan, a class of phenolic compounds, that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Polysaccharides
constituents.description: Polysaccharides from Schisandra chinensis are high-molecular-weight, water-soluble compounds composed of repeating units of monosaccharides, exhibiting antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Alkaloids
constituents.description: Schisandrine is a known alkaloid from Schisandra chinensis with sedative, anticonvulsant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Lignans
constituents.description: A type of polyphenolic compound found in Schisandra chinensis, exhibiting antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Schisandrone
constituents.description: A lignan with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

constituents.name: Schisandrol
constituents.description: Schisandrol is a lignan constituent of Schisandra chinensis, known for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties.

constituents.name: Schisandrin C
constituents.description: Schisandrin C is a lignan with antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Schisandrin A
constituents.description: A lignan, a type of tetracyclic triterpenoid with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Schisantherin A
constituents.description: Schisantherin A is a lignan with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Schisandrins B
constituents.description: Schisandrins B is a lignan constituent of Schisandra chinensis with antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties.

constituents.name: Schisantherin B
constituents.description: Schisantherin B is a lignan with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

constituents.name: Triterpenes
constituents.description: Triterpenes are a class of compounds found in Schisandra chinensis, characterized by their tricyclic carbon skeleton and have been reported to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties.

constituents.name: Schisantherin D
constituents.description: Schisantherin D is a lignan compound that has been found to possess antioxidant and free radical scavenging properties.

side_effects.side_effect_list_description: Stomach upset, nausea, diarrhea and vomiting occur in individuals consuming Schisandra chinensis.
Seizure activity occurs due to excessive GABA inhibitory effects caused by schizandrins in Schisandra chinensis
Liver enzyme elevations, including ALT and AST, have been reported in some individuals taking Schisandra chinensis.

Schisandra chinensis extracts cause a significant increase in blood pressure in some individuals due to their high content of schisandrins, which have a direct vasoconstrictive effect on blood vessels.
Gastrointestinal upset, including nausea and vomiting, occurs in individuals taking Schisandra chinensis.
Schisandra chinensis ingestion causes gastrointestinal irritation leading to abdominal pain.
Schisandra chinensis ingestion has been associated with gastrointestinal side effects, including diarrhea in some individuals.

Schisandra chinensis consumption has been associated with clinical hypothyroidism in animal studies, which can lead to increased levels of thyroid-stimulating hormone and decreased levels of thyroxine.
Schisandra chinensis consumption causes headaches due to its high content of schisandrins, which act as vasoconstrictors.
Consuming Schisandra chinensis in high doses can lead to disrupted sleep patterns.

intro_description: Schisandra chinensis, commonly known as schisandra, is a traditional Chinese herb used for its medicinal properties. It's primarily used to treat conditions such as cardiovascular disease, respiratory issues, and cognitive decline. The herb has health benefits like antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and anti-aging effects. It possesses therapeutic properties like adaptogenic, immunomodulatory, and neuroprotective effects. Main bioactive compounds include lignans, alkaloids, and phenolic acids. Schisandra is often prepared as a tea or dried fruit.

uses_list.description: This plant is used for fatigue because it contains compounds that may help increase energy levels and reduce oxidative stress, thereby alleviating symptoms of fatigue.
It is used for hypertension as its constituents may help lower blood pressure by regulating the balance of electrolytes in the body and promoting vasodilation.
This plant is used for diabetes because it may help regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity due to the presence of bioactive compounds that influence glucose metabolism.

It is used for memory loss as its constituents, particularly schisandrins, may help improve cognitive function and memory by protecting neurons from oxidative damage and promoting the growth of new neurons.
This plant is used for anxiety because it may help reduce stress and anxiety by regulating the balance of neurotransmitters, such as serotonin and dopamine, in the brain.
It is used for cancer as its bioactive compounds may help inhibit the growth of cancer cells, induce apoptosis, and prevent metastasis by modulating various signaling pathways.
This plant is used for anemia as its constituents may help increase red blood cell count and hemoglobin levels by stimulating the production of erythropoietin and improving iron metabolism.
It is used for digestive issues as its bioactive compounds may help improve digestion, reduce inflammation, and protect the gastrointestinal tract from oxidative damage.

This plant is used for cardiovascular disease as its constituents may help lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and prevent the formation of blood clots by regulating lipid metabolism and improving cardiovascular function.
It is used for liver disease as its bioactive compounds may help protect liver cells from oxidative damage, reduce inflammation, and improve liver function by modulating various signaling pathways.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Schisandra chinensis is used to treat fatigue, often a precursor to more serious conditions, by increasing energy levels and endurance. Additionally, it is used to alleviate anxiety, a major risk factor for various health issues, and hypertension, which can lead to cardiovascular disease. Furthermore, Schisandra chinensis has been found to help manage diabetes, a common comorbidity with cardiovascular disease, and has been shown to exhibit anticancer properties, potentially reducing the risk of cancer. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, and has been used to treat liver disease, which is often a result of digestive issues. Schisandra chinensis has also been found to improve memory loss, which can be associated with depression, and has been used to treat insomnia, stress, and atherosclerosis, all of which can be linked to cardiovascular disease, as well as hepatitis and stroke, and has been shown to boost the immune system, improve aging skin, and alleviate gastrointestinal issues.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional chinese medicine, Schisandra chinensis is used to treat fatigue and hypertension, its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties helping to alleviate symptoms. Schisandra chinensis is also used to address memory loss, anxiety, and diabetes, as it has been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce blood sugar levels. Additionally, it is used for cancer prevention and treatment, as well as cancer management, due to its ability to induce apoptosis and inhibit tumor growth. Furthermore, the plant is used to treat digestive issues, liver disease, anemia, depression, cardiovascular disease, hepatitis, and inflammation, as it has been found to improve liver function, increase red blood cell count, and reduce cardiovascular risk factors. Schisandra chinensis is also used to address asthma, cognitive decline, high blood pressure, insomnia, neurodegenerative diseases, and respiratory infections, showcasing its wide range of therapeutic applications.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Schisandra chinensis is used to treat mental fatigue, which is often associated with stress, by reducing oxidative stress and enhancing cognitive function. The plant is also used to manage diabetes, a condition characterized by insulin resistance and glucose intolerance, through its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve insulin sensitivity. Schisandra chinensis has been traditionally used to alleviate hypertension, a condition marked by high blood pressure, by reducing vascular resistance and promoting vasodilation. Furthermore, it is used to address anxiety, memory loss, cancer, liver disease, digestive issues, inflammation, anemia, hepatitis, atherosclerosis, cardiovascular disease, depression, kidney stones, and aging, as well as cancer prevention, sleep disorder, and respiratory infections, through its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. By mitigating these conditions, Schisandra chinensis is considered a valuable herbal remedy in ayurvedic medicine.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Schisandra chinensis is used to treat fatigue, which is often a symptom of anemia, a condition that can be alleviated by the plant's antioxidant properties. It is also used to manage diabetes, a condition closely related to cardiovascular disease, which can be prevented or alleviated by the plant's anti-inflammatory and cardiovascular protective effects. Additionally, it is used to lower high blood pressure and prevent hypertension, conditions often associated with liver disease, which can be treated with Schisandra chinensis's hepatoprotective properties. Furthermore, the plant is used to improve memory loss and prevent neurodegenerative disease, conditions that can be linked to oxidative stress, which is mitigated by the plant's antioxidant properties. It is also used to treat digestive issues, asthma, anxiety, stress, hepatitis, inflammation, gastritis, atherosclerosis, a weakened immune system, osteoporosis, and cancer prevention.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Schisandra chinensis is used to treat fatigue, and its adaptogenic properties help alleviate stress, a significant contributor to anxiety and cognitive decline. Research suggests that Schisandra chinensis may also be beneficial in managing hypertension, diabetes, and anemia by regulating blood sugar levels and improving iron absorption. Additionally, it has been studied for its potential to prevent cancer, atherosclerosis, and cardiovascular disease by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress. Schisandra chinensis has also been found to exhibit hepatoprotective effects, which can help alleviate liver disease, hepatitis, and digestive issues. Furthermore, it may be used to address memory loss, erectile dysfunction, aging skin, and neurodegenerative disease, including arthritis, through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains a combination of adaptogenic compounds, including lignans and saponins, which can help regulate the body's response to stress by inhibiting the production of stress hormones like cortisol.

The plant's lignans, particularly schisandrins, have been shown to improve memory and cognitive function by enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters and increasing the growth of new neurons in the brain.

The plant's adaptogenic compounds can help improve cognitive function by regulating the body's response to stress, improving memory, and enhancing the activity of neurotransmitters involved in learning and memory.
The plant's lignans have been shown to have hepatoprotective effects, supporting liver function and reducing liver damage caused by toxins and oxidative stress.
The plant's adaptogenic compounds can help increase energy levels by regulating the body's response to stress, improving sleep quality, and enhancing physical performance.
The plant's lignans and saponins have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, boosting the immune system's response to infection and inflammation.
The plant's lignans and saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation caused by oxidative stress, injury, or infection.
The plant's saponins and lignans may help improve digestion by reducing inflammation in the gut, improving gut health, and enhancing the absorption of nutrients.
The plant's lignans and saponins have antioxidant properties, reducing oxidative stress caused by free radicals and protecting cells from damage.
The plant's lignans and saponins may help protect against cell damage and death caused by oxidative stress, inflammation, and other forms of cellular stress.

benefits_description: Schisandra chinensis reduces stress by inhibiting the activity of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis, thereby decreasing the production of cortisol. It improves memory through its antioxidant properties, which protect the brain from oxidative damage and promote the health of neurons. Schisandra chinensis enhances cognitive function by increasing the production of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and acetylcholine, and improving blood flow to the brain. The plant supports liver health by enhancing the activity of glutathione S-transferase, a key enzyme involved in detoxification, and reducing the accumulation of lipids in liver cells. Schisandra chinensis increases energy by stimulating the activity of mitochondria, the cellular structures responsible for energy production, and enhances the utilization of ATP. Schisandra chinensis boosts immune function by increasing the production of cytokines, which are signaling molecules that coordinate the immune response. The plant reduces inflammation by inhibiting the activity of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and lipoxygenase, and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Schisandra chinensis improves digestion by stimulating the activity of digestive enzymes, such as amylase and lipase, and enhancing the absorption of nutrients. The antioxidant properties of Schisandra chinensis reduce oxidative damage by neutralizing free radicals and protecting cellular components from oxidative stress. Schisandra chinensis protects against cancer by inhibiting the growth of cancer cells and inducing apoptosis, or programmed cell death.
benefits_circulatory_system_description: Schisandra chinensis improves blood flow by increasing vasodilation, which is attributed to its vasodilatory and anti-inflammatory properties. It lowers blood pressure due to its antioxidant and anti-hypertensive effects, which reduce oxidative stress and improve vascular function. Enhanced cardiac output is achieved through its cardiotonic properties, allowing the heart to pump more efficiently. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis reduces inflammation and boosts antioxidants, protecting the cardiovascular system from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, thereby protecting against clotting. By improving lipid profiles, reducing cardiovascular stress, and enhancing microcirculation, Schisandra chinensis helps maintain a healthy circulatory system, ultimately reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Schisandra chinensis regulates digestion by modulating gut microbiota and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, thereby inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria. Boosting gut health, it stimulates the growth of beneficial microorganisms, such as Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium, which are essential for a balanced gut ecosystem. Soothing gut inflammation, Schisandra chinensis has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that contribute to inflammation. Improving gut motility, it enhances the movement of food through the digestive tract, preventing conditions such as constipation and bowel obstruction. Protecting gut microbiome, stimulating gut lining, reducing gut bloating, enhancing gut absorption, and healing gut ulcers are also benefits of this plant, attributed to its antioxidant and anti-ulcer properties.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Schisandra chinensis regulates adrenal function and cortisol production by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which help mitigate oxidative stress. It improves insulin sensitivity by possessing anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the pancreatic tissue and enhance the uptake of glucose. Stabilizing cortisol levels and supporting thyroid function are also attributed to Schisandra chinensis, which exhibits adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and maintain homeostasis. The plant's ability to enhance pancreas function and balance hormone levels is due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect the pancreatic tissue and reduce inflammation in the endocrine system. By exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, Schisandra chinensis supports metabolism, reduces inflammation, improves glucose uptake, and protects the endocrine system from oxidative stress and damage.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Schisandra chinensis protects skin by exerting its antimicrobial properties, preventing the growth of pathogenic microorganisms that can lead to skin infections. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties repair tissues damaged by free radicals and inflammation, promoting a faster recovery from skin injuries. This plant enhances elasticity due to its ability to stimulate collagen production, a crucial protein for maintaining skin firmness and suppleness. Schisandra chinensis reduces inflammation by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and fights oxidation by scavenging free radicals and neutralizing reactive oxygen species. The plant improves hydration by increasing the production of aquaporin-3, a protein responsible for water transport across cell membranes, and boosts collagen by stimulating fibroblast proliferation, which slows aging, reduces scarring, and stimulates growth.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Schisandra chinensis boosts lymph flow due to its high content of lignans and triterpenes, which increase the activity of lymphatic vessels and enhance the transport of lymph fluid. This plant stimulates lymph nodes by exhibiting antimicrobial properties that reduce the risk of infection and inflammation in lymphoid tissues, thereby promoting a healthy immune response. Schisandra chinensis supports detoxification through its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which protect the body from oxidative stress and promote the removal of toxins. The lignans present in Schisandra chinensis also enhance immune function by activating immune cells and increasing the production of cytokines, which play a crucial role in fighting infections. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis improves lymphatic circulation by relaxing the smooth muscle of lymphatic vessels and reducing lymph swelling due to its anti-inflammatory effects, while increasing lymph protein and improving lymph filtration by enhancing the activity of lymphatic enzymes and stabilizing lymph tissue through its antioxidant properties, and finally, activates lymph cells by promoting the production of lymphokines.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Schisandra chinensis reduce joint pain due to its anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes that exacerbate joint inflammation. Strengthen bones are a result of Schisandra chinensis' ability to stimulate osteoblast activity, promoting bone formation and increasing bone density. Relieve muscle spasms is a benefit of Schisandra chinensis' antispasmodic properties, which relax muscle contractions and reduce muscle tension. Improve bone density is attributed to Schisandra chinensis' antioxidant properties, which protect bones from oxidative damage and promote bone health. Schisandra chinensis promote bone regeneration by stimulating the expression of genes involved in bone formation and inhibiting the expression of genes involved in bone resorption, ultimately preventing osteoporosis and enhancing muscle function, while also soothe musculoskeletal inflammation and improve mobility range, reduce muscle fatigue.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Schisandra chinensis enhances memory by activating the acetylcholinesterase enzyme, increasing the production of acetylcholine, a neurotransmitter crucial for learning and memory. This plant supports neuroplasticity by stimulating the growth of new neurons and increasing the expression of genes involved in neural adaptation. Schisandra chinensis reduces anxiety by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters such as GABA, serotonin, and dopamine, which play a key role in regulating emotional responses. Its adaptogenic properties improve focus by increasing the production of dopamine and norepinephrine, which enhance attention and motivation. Schisandra chinensis protects neurons from oxidative stress and inflammation by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, while relieving stress and promoting relaxation by regulating the body's response to stress.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Schisandra chinensis improves fertility by enhancing ovulation and supporting hormone balance due to its antioxidant and adaptogenic properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and promote a healthy reproductive environment. It also boosts libido and increases testosterone levels in both men and women, resulting from its adaptogenic and androgenic properties that stimulate the production of sex hormones and improve sexual function. Furthermore, Schisandra chinensis strengthens reproductive organs by exerting anti-inflammatory effects, which reduce inflammation and oxidative damage that can impair reproductive health. Additionally, it regulates menstrual cycle and protects fetal development by acting as an antioxidant, which scavenges free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and harm the developing fetus. Its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties also enhance sperm quality by reducing oxidative damage and inflammation in the reproductive tract.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Schisandra chinensis reduces inflammation through its anti-inflammatory properties, making it an effective treatment for various respiratory issues. Preventing bronchitis is also a significant health benefit of Schisandra chinensis, attributed to its ability to stimulate the production of mucus, which helps to clear out pathogens from the respiratory tract. This plant relaxes airways by increasing nitric oxide production, a potent vasodilator that helps to widen the airways and improve breathing. Boosting immunity is another essential health benefit, achieved through the stimulation of the immune system by Schisandra chinensis's immunomodulatory properties. By possessing antimicrobial and antifungal properties, Schisandra chinensis fights infections, sooths coughs, eases congestion, protects lungs, removes mucus, and calms asthma, making it a valuable natural remedy for respiratory health.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Schisandra chinensis stimulate bladder function by increasing the contractions of the bladder wall, improving its ability to hold and release urine efficiently. It protect kidney health through its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress that can damage kidney cells. Schisandra chinensis reduce urinary frequency by regulating the autonomic nervous system, which controls the release of urine. Its anti-inflammatory properties improve urine flow by reducing inflammation and swelling in the urinary tract, allowing urine to pass smoothly. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis treat kidney stones by inhibiting the formation of oxalate crystals, a common component of kidney stones, and relieve urinary tract infections due to its antimicrobial properties, which kill bacteria that cause infection; it also prevent kidney disease by slowing the progression of chronic kidney disease and support prostate health by inhibiting the growth of prostate cancer cells.

properties_list.description: Schisandra chinensis has antioxidant properties due to its high content of phenolic compounds, which can neutralize free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage.

Schisandra chinensis has anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as arthritis and gout.

Schisandra chinensis has antimicrobial properties, which are effective against a range of microorganisms, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, due to its ability to inhibit the growth and replication of these pathogens.

Schisandra chinensis has analgesic properties, which are attributed to its ability to reduce pain perception and alleviate conditions such as headaches and migraines, although the exact mechanism is not fully understood.

Schisandra chinensis has antiviral properties, which are effective against a range of viruses, including influenza and herpes simplex, due to its ability to inhibit viral replication and reduce the severity of viral infections.

Schisandra chinensis has cardioprotective properties, which are attributed to its ability to reduce blood pressure, improve lipid profiles, and prevent cardiovascular disease by inhibiting the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Schisandra chinensis has neuroprotective properties, which are attributed to its ability to reduce oxidative stress, inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, and prevent the formation of beta-amyloid plaques, thereby alleviating conditions such as Alzheimer's disease and Parkinson's disease.

Schisandra chinensis has immunomodulatory properties, which are attributed to its ability to regulate immune function and prevent the overactivation of immune cells, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating conditions such as autoimmune diseases.

Schisandra chinensis has hepatoprotective properties, which are attributed to its ability to reduce liver damage and prevent the progression of liver disease, although the exact mechanism is not fully understood.

Schisandra chinensis has anticancer properties, which are attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth and metastasis of cancer cells, although the exact mechanism is not fully understood and more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.

parts_description: The most used parts of Schisandra chinensis for medicinal purposes are fruits, roots, seeds, and rhizomes. Fruits contain flavonoids, lignans, and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties. Roots are rich in schisandrins, which are lignans with neuroprotective, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Seeds contain saponins and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, while rhizomes are a source of schisandrins, lignans with neuroprotective and anti-inflammatory effects. These parts have been used to treat various health conditions, including fatigue, cardiovascular disease, and cognitive decline, due to their combined antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory properties.

parts_list.name: fruits
parts_list.description: The fruits of this plant are rich in bioactive compounds such as schisandrins, schisandrins B and C, and lignans, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, contributing to their potential health benefits in cardiovascular health, cognitive function, and immune system regulation.

parts_list.name: roots
parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain schisandrins, gomisin A, and other lignans, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, and have been traditionally used to treat respiratory, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular disorders, as well as to enhance immune function and reduce oxidative stress.

parts_list.name: seeds
parts_list.description: The seeds of this plant are a rich source of schisandrins, gomisin A, and other lignans, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, and have been traditionally used to treat respiratory, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular disorders, as well as to enhance immune function and reduce oxidative stress.

parts_list.name: rhizomes
parts_list.description: The rhizomes of this plant contain schisandrins, gomisin A, and other lignans, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory, gastrointestinal, and cardiovascular disorders, as well as to enhance immune function and reduce oxidative stress, and have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat fatigue, improve cognitive function, and enhance physical performance by supporting immune system and cardiovascular health, and has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_overview: Schisandra chinensis capsule is a herbal preparation used to treat various health conditions including fatigue, anxiety, digestive issues, depression, sleep disorders, inflammation, cancer prevention, hypertension, and diabetes. It is one of the most commonly used herbal supplements worldwide due to its proven efficacy and wide availability. The frequent reported effect of this preparation is improved overall well-being. Schisandra chinensis capsule is made from the fruits and roots of the Schisandra chinensis plant. The manufacturing process is moderately complex, requiring careful processing to extract the active compounds from the plant materials.
preparation_recipe: Gather Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots from a trusted source.
Dry the collected Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots in a warm, well-ventilated area to remove excess moisture.
Grind the dried Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots into a fine powder using a grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots in a specific ratio to achieve the desired herbal blend.
Fill gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the Schisandra chinensis herbal blend and seal them tightly to preserve the medicinal properties.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat conditions such as fatigue, anxiety, insomnia, and cardiovascular disease, and to support liver and kidney function, as well as to improve cognitive function and immune system function.
preparations.preparation_overview: Schisandra chinensis tincture is a herbal preparation made from the fruits and roots of Schisandra chinensis, a plant native to Asia. This tincture is commonly used to treat fatigue, stress, memory loss, anxiety, sleep disorder, liver disease, hypertension, digestive issues, cardiovascular disease and cancer. It's widely used due to its availability and established efficacy. The common effect of taking Schisandra chinensis tincture is improved cognitive function and reduced stress levels. The tincture is made by infusing the plant's fruits and roots in a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin, and then concentrating the resulting liquid to create a potent extract. The process of making this tincture is moderately difficult, requiring careful selection of plant material, precise dosing and controlled extraction methods.
preparation_recipe: Gather Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots from a reputable source.
Clean and dry the Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots to prevent contamination.
Combine the dried Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots in a clean glass jar to create a 1:1 ratio.
Fill a glass container with a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin, and then pour the solvent over the Schisandra chinensis mixture.
Steep the Schisandra chinensis mixture in the solvent for 2-6 weeks, shaking the container regularly to facilitate extraction.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Decoction made from this plant is used to treat liver and kidney disorders, improve cardiovascular health, and enhance cognitive function, and is also used as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent.
preparations.preparation_overview: Schisandra chinensis decoction is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat various health conditions, including fatigue, anemia, diabetes, hypertension, asthma, gastritis, impotence, and cancer. It's a common preparation in traditional medicine, widely used in China, Russia, and other parts of Asia. Schisandra chinensis decoction has a common effect of enhancing physical endurance and mental clarity, improving overall health and well-being. This preparation is typically made from the fruits, roots, and seeds of the Schisandra chinensis plant, which contain bioactive compounds like schisandrins and lignans. The process of making this decoction is moderately difficult, requiring precise measurement of ingredients and proper cooking techniques to ensure optimal potency and efficacy.
preparation_recipe: Gather 1:1 ratio of Schisandra chinensis fruits and roots, and a small amount of seeds.
Rinse the Schisandra chinensis fruits, roots, and seeds thoroughly with cold water.
Combine the cleaned Schisandra chinensis fruits, roots, and seeds in a pot and add sufficient water to cover them.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for a specified time.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids, then let it cool to room temperature before use.

preparations.preparation_list_description: Tea made from this plant is used to improve liver function, boost immune system, increase physical endurance and reduce stress and fatigue.
preparations.preparation_overview: Schisandra chinensis tea is a traditional preparation used to treat fatigue, stress, anxiety, inflammation, digestive issues, hypertension, sleep disorders, depression and immune system problems. It's one of the most common herbal teas consumed globally, widely available in many health food stores and online platforms. Regular consumption of Schisandra chinensis tea often leads to improved mental clarity and reduced stress levels, a common effect experienced by users. The preparation is made from the fruits, roots and seeds of the Schisandra chinensis plant, which are typically combined in a specific ratio to create a tea blend. Making Schisandra chinensis tea is relatively easy, as it involves steeping the plant material in hot water for a few minutes, then straining and serving.
preparation_recipe: Gather and clean Schisandra chinensis fruits, roots, and seeds.
Dry the cleaned fruits, roots, and seeds in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried fruits, roots, and seeds into a fine powder.
Mix the ground powder with boiling water in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for a few minutes before straining and serving.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Schisandra chinensis are experience gastrointestinal disturbances, show signs of liver damage, and experience nausea and vomiting, which can be severe if the plant is ingested in large quantities or for extended periods. Additionally, some individuals may develop seizures, a rare but serious side effect that requires immediate medical attention. Improper use of Schisandra chinensis can also induce abdominal pain, increase blood pressure, and lead to diarrhea, exacerbating underlying health conditions. Furthermore, it may cause headaches and insomnia, disrupting normal sleep patterns and daily life. Thyroid problems, although rare, are another potential side effect of improperly using Schisandra chinensis, highlighting the importance of proper preparation and dosage.

precautions.precaution_list_description: Schisandra chinensis may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners, diabetes medications, and stimulants, and may have adverse effects in people with certain medical conditions, including liver or kidney disease, and pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Schisandra chinensis may lower blood pressure due to its high antioxidant content and lignan compounds, which have been shown to cause a decrease in systolic blood pressure in hypertensive patients.
Schisandra chinensis should be avoided by pregnant and breastfeeding women due to lack of safety data.
Schisandra chinensis may interact with blood thinners, increasing the risk of bleeding.
Schisandra chinensis contains compounds that can irritate the gastrointestinal tract and exacerbate existing digestive problems.
Excessive consumption may cause liver damage, gastrointestinal upset, and other adverse effects.
Schisandra chinensis use may cause skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals.
Schisandra chinensis may decrease blood sugar levels, potentially altering the effect of diabetes medications.
Due to lack of safety and efficacy data in pediatric populations, Schisandra chinensis is not recommended for children.
Regularly monitor liver function tests due to potential hepatotoxicity associated with Schisandra chinensis.

intro_study: According to a study published by "Ceska a Slovenska farmacie : casopis Ceske farmaceuticke spolecnosti a Slovenske farmaceuticke spolecnosti", Schisandra chinensis has been found to have several beneficial effects. It has been used to treat cardiovascular diseases, liver diseases, and diseases related to the central nervous system associated with aging. Additionally, Schisandra chinensis extracts have been used as a supplement in the treatment of various conditions, including neoplasms, diabetes, and to promote oral hygiene and skin and hair care.

According to a study published by "Frontiers in plant science", Schisandra chinensis has shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of compounds such as gomisin D, schisandrol B, schisantherin C, kadsuranin, and kadlongilactone F in its roots. These compounds contribute to the plant's biological activity, which was found to be higher in the roots compared to the fruit. The roots of Schisandra chinensis could serve as a new medicinal source with potential benefits for health.

According to a study published by "Applied microbiology and biotechnology", Schisandra chinensis is a rich source of therapeutically useful schisandra lignans, which could have significant benefits for medical and cosmetic industries. The plant contains high amounts of schisandrin, angeloylgomisin Q, gomisin A, and angeloylgomisin H, which are compounds that may contribute to its potential health benefits. The findings suggest that Schisandra chinensis could be a valuable resource for the production of these beneficial compounds.

According to a study published by "Journal of natural medicines", it has been found that Schisandra chinensis can stimulate skeletal muscle cell proliferation, leading to increased muscle strength. This effect is attributed to the compounds schisacaulin D and alismoxide, which enhance the expression of myosin heavy chain (MyHC) and increase the number of fused myotubes. As a result, Schisandra chinensis may be a potential candidate for treating sarcopenia, a condition characterized by muscle weakness and wasting.

According to a study published by "Ceska a Slovenska farmacie : casopis Ceske farmaceuticke spolecnosti a Slovenske farmaceuticke spolecnosti", Schisandra chinensis has been found to have several beneficial effects, particularly as a hepatoprotective agent, helping to protect the liver from damage. Additionally, it has been identified as an adaptogenic and antioxidative agent, suggesting its ability to aid in stress relief and neutralize free radicals in the body. The study also highlights that the complex mixture of its principal constituents, including lignans such as gomisin A and gomisin N, work together to produce a synergistic and potent therapeutic effect.
properties_description: The therapeutic properties of Schisandra chinensis are multifaceted, with its antioxidant capacity attributed to its rich content of lignans and phenolic acids, which enable it to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The anti-inflammatory activity of Schisandra chinensis is associated with its saponin and flavonoid content, which can modulate inflammatory responses and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of this plant are due to the presence of lignans and terpenoids, which can inhibit the growth of various microorganisms and prevent infections. Schisandra chinensis also exhibits analgesic and antiviral properties, with its lignans and flavonoids shown to inhibit the activity of pain-related enzymes and viral replication, respectively. Furthermore, its cardioprotective, neuroprotective, immunomodulatory, hepatoprotective, and anticancer properties are attributed to its ability to reduce oxidative stress, modulate immune responses, and inhibit the growth of cancer cells, all of which are supported by its rich content of bioactive compounds, including lignans, flavonoids, and saponins.
constituents_description: The healing constituents of Schisandra chinensis are schisandrins, a type of lignan with antioxidant and adaptogenic properties, saponins that have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, phenolic acids including ferulic and sinapic acid that have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, and terpenoids, including schisantherin and schisandrin B, that display antioxidant and anti-inflammatory actions, all of which contribute to the herb's purported adaptogenic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The lignans in Schisandra chinensis are thought to be responsible for its adaptogenic properties, allowing it to protect against various forms of cellular stress. The saponins in the herb have been shown to modulate the immune system and exert anti-inflammatory effects. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Schisandra chinensis have been demonstrated to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may contribute to the herb's purported ability to protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. The terpenoids in the herb have been found to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may contribute to its purported adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.
preparation_description: The most common herbal preparation of Schisandra chinensis for medicinal purposes are capsules, which contain the dried fruit or berries and are used to improve cognitive function, memory, and physical performance. A tincture is another preparation made from the extract of the fruit, often used to treat liver and kidney diseases, as well as to boost the immune system. Decoctions are made by boiling the fruit in water and are traditionally used to treat respiratory problems and fever. Tea, a popular preparation made by infusing the dried fruit in hot water, is commonly consumed to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve sleep quality. These medicinal preparations of Schisandra chinensis have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for centuries to treat various health conditions.
precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Schisandra chinensis medicinally are to consult a healthcare professional to discuss potential benefits and risks, as the herb may interact with blood thinners, such as warfarin, and affect blood pressure levels, which should be closely monitored to prevent complications. Additionally, individuals should avoid using Schisandra chinensis during pregnancy and breastfeeding due to a lack of safety data, and may need to use it in moderation to avoid exacerbating existing digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome. Furthermore, it is essential to be aware of the potential for allergic reactions, which can range from mild skin irritation to severe anaphylaxis, and to monitor liver function tests, as the herb may cause liver damage in rare cases. Schisandra chinensis may also interact with diabetes medications, such as metformin, and is not recommended for children due to limited research on its safety and efficacy in this population.
